title: Maximum Width of Binary Tree
hints: Assign each node an index as if the tree were a perfect binary tree. The root is 0, and a node at index i has children at 2i+1 and 2i+2.
Perform a level-order traversal (BFS) while keeping track of each node's index. For each level, calculate the width using the indices of the first and last nodes.
To prevent integer overflow with deep trees, normalize the indices at each level by subtracting the index of the leftmost node from all nodes in that level before processing children.
